_comment: Canned triager responses (record/replay) for the --mock backend. Keyed by cluster_signature (sorted top-4 keywords). Hand-vetted; this is what `make demo` and the golden test replay so CI is FREE + DETERMINISTIC (no live Claude call). A signature not found here falls back to fb_os.triager._heuristic_decision so the loop still closes.

theme.summary: Users want to attach ONE specific past session to /feedback by scope, not a coarse time window — a privacy need on sensitive/shared code.

question.question: Are you also wishing /feedback could attach a single past session by scope, instead of only a time window?
question.hypothesis: Users on sensitive or shared code want per-session attach scope, not just a 24h/7d window.

theme.summary: Redaction is firing on harmless output (bash results, public URLs, error text) — users feel it strips too much and want per-category control.

question.question: When redaction masked something, was it actually harmless output you'd have been fine sharing?
question.hypothesis: The redaction floor over-strips harmless content; per-category tuning would raise feedback usefulness without lowering safety.

theme.summary: The proactive watcher prompts to capture feedback too often; users want a single quiet offer per session that remembers a dismissal.

question.question: Would a single 'capture this?' offer per session (silent after you dismiss it) feel right, instead of repeated prompts?
question.hypothesis: One quiet, dismissable offer per session removes the nag without losing the high-signal moments.

theme.summary: The Super+V voice-confirm has noticeable latency between keypress and transcription, breaking flow.

question.question: Is the lag between pressing Super+V and seeing the transcription the main thing slowing your voice-confirm flow?
question.hypothesis: Local whisper transcription latency is the dominant friction in the voice-confirm path.
